entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 22 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-22 07:09AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Leigh Sales' favourite interviews
  • Kiss Kiss by Holly Valance
  • Power industry fires warning as states splinter on landmark reform
  • Two killed, two injured in horror Brisbane crash
  • Australia news LIVE: Crown Sydney receives conditional licence to open; RBA warns of further interest rate rises; NSW, ACT teacher strike set for June 30
  • Hundreds killed and injured in eastern Afghanistan earthquake
  • ASX falls as US futures weigh on the market, wiping out early gains
  • Ratten confirms Saints interest in De Goey
  • Former Sydney swim coach arrested
  • Victoria police arrest alleged serial hoon
  • High steaks bust: $2000 stolen meat uncovered in Melbourne
  • Blackouts reduce as nations energy crisis eases
  • Man stabbed in road rage attack in Sydney’s West
  • TikTok star admits defying COVID restrictions at anti-lockdown rally
  • Inquiry into John Barilaro's US trade role
  • Thousands of commuters stranded around Victoria after V/Line communications fault
  • Unvaccinated teachers to be welcomed back to schools as mandate ends
  • Gus previews Origin 2: Six Tackles with Gus - Episode 17
  • Massive lines, painful delays at Heathrow airports
  • ‘I miss Tom’: The only thing that could have topped Jake’s NSW recall
  • ‘Failed veterans’: Ex-minister grilled over tardy response to claims backlog
  • Electricity market suspension to be lifted as supply surges back
  • Best of cartoons, June 22, 2022
  • Why interest rates are going up, and won’t be coming down
  • Energy spin can be powerful, but rhetoric won’t keep the lights on
  • Minister puts gas exporters on notice
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Future of telehealth uncertain as doctors call for Medicare rebates to be locked in
  • Universities on workplace watchdog’s wage theft priority list
  • Best of cartoons, June 21, 2022
  • ‘Fundamentally flawed, prone to abuse’: Dire warning on cryptos’ future
  • Extraordinary electricity market shutdown may be lifted by Wednesday night
  • Veterans’ Affairs lost $430m after former minister forced to make cuts
  • Planner tells ICAC he was ‘stunned’ when council approved project against advice
  • From Rose Bay to riot squad raid: police target alleged Dark Web dealers
  • Renowned swim coach Dick Caine granted bail over historical sexual abuse charges
  • When you fail teachers, you fail children
  • NSW Premier seeks advice on Barilaro’s US posting
  • Wollongong man charged after allegedly pouring petrol over child
  • Author painted Chris Dawson as a killer in unpublished book, trial hears
  • Newlywed identified after death in freak honeymoon accident
  • ‘A scandalous waste of money’: $25m spend for Bridge flagpole must be avoided
  • In the Herald: June 22, 1964
  • Breakfast broadcast with added polish
  • The NSW budget in five charts
  • We asked the Treasurer to invest in the future of women. He delivered.
  • NSW racing industry lands funding boost in gambling tax shakeup
  • Teachers to hold ‘unprecedented’ strike as anger over shortages, pay boils over
  • ‘I will not break my oath’: Officials testify about defying Trump’s orders
  • She held the line against Trump’s ‘stolen election’ plot; now she pays the price
  • Damning evidence slams police response in Uvalde shooting
  • ‘Abject failure’: Police made ‘terrible’ decisions during Uvalde shooting, official says
  • Bill Cosby found guilty in civil trial
  • ‘Wrong decision’: Germany to defy EU plan to ban petrol cars by 2035
  • Bill Cosby found liable for sexual assault at Playboy Mansion in 1975
  • Druids, pagans flock to Stonehenge for first pandemic-era solstice ceremony
  • US January 6th Attack committee hearing
  • Ukraine's secret, deadly rescue missions
  • US rolls out COVID-19 vaccines for youngest kids
  • Plane catches fire after crash landing at Miami International Airport
  • Woman forced to wear gas mask to protect from neighbour's coal fire
  • Australian Bushmasters in Ukraine
  • UK to offer monkeypox vaccines to some gay, bisexual men
  • Former UK High Commissioner George Brandis appointed ANU national security professor
  • Russia threatens Lithuania with ‘serious negative impacts’ over blocked rail
  • Stephen Colbert says Late Show staffers guilty of ‘first-degree puppetry’
  • Fatal motorbike crash closes three M1 lanes, two dead in separate crash
  • Earthquake kills more than 250 people in Afghanistan and Pakistan
  • Desire to bounce to the top prompted arson campaign, court hold
  • Warning on further Metro Tunnel cost blowouts in next ‘risky phase’
  • Parents of missing autistic teen plead for help as search enters fifth day
  • Carol Raye: actor leaves legacy of stage and television work
  • Is the era of cheap money over?
  • Grocery delivery startup Voly slashes operations as tech sector slumps
  • Humm deal with Latitude still possible, say analysts
  • Perth casino operators to face $100 million fine for dodgy behaviour
  • The ‘crypto winter’ has set in but it may have a silver lining
  • Crown gets green light to open Sydney casino
  • ‘Very appealing’: US vaccine giant looks to amp up Australian trials
  • The weakness of Putin’s economic show of force
  • Elon Musk sounds off on ‘inevitable’ recession, Twitter deal and Trump
  • Big-four bank mortgage market share raided as refinancing booms
  • How a WA gas project could impact the world’s most famous reef
  • Ovolo hotels eyes further growth as tourism sector shakes off the pandemic
  • Jealous of everyone’s European summer? Here are seven shows to help beat the winter blues
  • Older, wiser, more empathetic: The Chaser team reunites for one last laugh
  • Looking for life? You might find it in these tuning forks, artist says
  • Bonnie & Clyde review: A little murder, mayhem and song, anyone?
  • Who’s laughing now? Barry is game-changing TV with a point to prove
  • Elvis review: A tedious fever dream at times, but more compelling than Gatsby
  • The revival of the Anzac play the censors wouldn’t let us see 60 years ago
  • Michael Parkinson’s interviews are fascinating TV, even with the new warning
  • From the Archives, 1982: It’s all Go-Go in the battle to the top

  • Cameras attached to tiger sharks
  • National weather forecast for Wednesday June 22
  • ‘Shark cams’ reveal Ningaloo sea turtles freeze to escape predators’ gaze
  • La Niña 'over' but weather pattern may re-form later this year, BoM says
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.content:

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.content:

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.title: Guardian: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war latest: ‘significant’ blow against Snake Island occupiers, says Ukrainian army – live
  • A Woman’s Game by Suzanne Wrack review – taking back the pitch
  • The horribly twee adventures of Commander Toto – Space Cavoodle | First Dog on the Moon
  • Red-carpet criticisms of Queensland’s premier reveal – yet again – a gender double standard | Van Badham
  • A moment that changed me: a car crash altered my mother for ever – and I was sent back to boarding school
  • Get up, walk the dog – my life is mundane, but because I’m gay Texas Republicans think I’m abnormal | Arwa Mahdawi
  • I took the train to Düsseldorf – here’s my guide to the city
  • ‘It felt like wringing a dry sponge’: India’s dairy farmers face searing heat
  • China floods force tens of thousands to evacuate with more rainfall expected
  • Afghanistan earthquake: at least 250 dead after 6.1 magnitude quake, state media says
  • Could the fake electors scheme prove to be Trump campaign’s Achilles’ heel?
  • Integrate, my mum said, so I did. But Eric Morecambe helped me find my Windrush self | Lenny Henry
  • Windrush: only one in four applicants have received compensation
  • Half in UK back genome editing to prevent severe diseases
  • Somalia: ‘The worst humanitarian crisis we’ve ever seen’
  • ‘I’ve had 10 affairs – I wasn’t that sort of woman when I arrived on Corrie!’ Britain’s longest serving soap stars tell all
  • Five rail workers on the strike: ‘There is no justification for us to get poorer every year’
  • ‘An embassy for optimism’: the legendary Africa Centre rises again
  • Neither citizens nor foreigners: Hongkongers struggle to adapt to life in Taiwan
  • A liberating high comes with group nudity at Dark Mofo – it can turn anyone into a giggling fool | Sian Cain
  • Texas shooting: school in Uvalde where 21 were killed will be demolished, says mayor
  • Australia funds GPS trackers on Sri Lankan fishing boats, partially to deter people smugglers
  • Taiwan scrambles jets to warn away Chinese planes in its air zone as tensions simmer
  • Russia-Ukraine war: what we know on day 119 of the invasion
  • ‘We want justice, not fuel’: Sri Lanka’s Tamils on north-south divide
  • Airplane catches on fire at Miami airport after landing gear collapses
  • Joe Lycett standup joke investigated by police after complaint
  • Facebook to axe ‘discriminatory’ algorithm in US government settlement
  • Love of the Irish: why are Gaelic games so popular in Asia?
  • Brexit is making cost of living crisis worse, new study claims
  • Bill Cosby found guilty of sexually abusing minor at Playboy Mansion
  • ‘There’s nowhere I feel safe’: Georgia elections workers describe how Trump upended their lives
  • January 6 hearings: state officials testify on Trump pressure to discredit election
  • Serena Williams makes winning return but remains coy about long-term plans
  • Dom Phillips was ‘collateral damage’ in drunken ambush, claims Brazil vice-president
  • Romelu Lukaku set to leave Chelsea and rejoin Inter after loan fee agreed
  • Four-time Super Bowl champion Rob Gronkowski retires from NFL for second time
  • Elon Musk’s daughter legally changes name and cuts ties with her father
  • Jonny Bairstow’s innings showed benefit of giving players a clear message | Mark Ramprakash
  • Tokyo mayoral win a ‘huge surprise’ for candidate living in Belgium
  • Covid surges across Europe as experts warn not let guard down
  • Macron holds talks with opposition over French parliamentary majority
  • Police ‘aggressive and provocative’ in Paris, Liverpool fans’ groups tell hearing
  • Conservationists in court bid to halt $16bn Scarborough gas project citing damage to barrier reef
  • Arsenal complete signing of ‘special talent’ Fábio Vieira from Porto
  • My partner will only have sex with me if I wear stockings and high heels
  • Lawyer says 20 of 24 lawsuits against Deshaun Watson have been settled
  • Morikawa denies he will join LIV Series as Koepka linked to Saudi-backed league
  • Ed Sheeran and co-writers awarded £900,000 in costs over copyright case
  • From J-Lo to Taylor Swift, ‘pop docs’ are glamorous fun – and smart propaganda | Hannah Strong
  • Less of Ewan McGregor please! The big problem with Obi-Wan Kenobi
  • ‘I think I would win’: Donald Trump takes aim at Ron DeSantis
  • Once banned, now knighted: how Horace Ové became the godfather of black British film-making
  • The French are obsessed with the burkini – and it’s all getting a bit embarrassing | Arwa Mahdawi
  • Russia threatens ‘serious consequences’ as Lithuania blocks rail goods
  • Which oils are best for cooking? | Kitchen aide
  • Eastern Donbas ‘extremely difficult’ for Ukrainians as Russia intensifies attacks
  • Nigel Slater’s recipe for courgettes with gorgonzola sauce
  • Salmon firm’s plan to fly fish in its own Boeing 757 alarms campaigners

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 22 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-22 06:09AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Power supply ‘fully restored’ in Hong Kong districts hit by worst outage in decade but some estates still waiting for electricity
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong tour group heads to Japan for the first time in more than 2 years after Tokyo eases Covid rules
  • What happened to Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant? Everything you need to know about the ill-fated tourist attraction
  • Hong Kong’s housing and economic development must be linked to the environment to create a liveable city
  • Unpacking Hong Kong’s laws, 25 years after handover: 5 rulings on Basic Law where Beijing, not the city’s courts, had final say in key cases
  • Hong Kong maritime authorities request report from owner after Jumbo Floating Restaurant sinks in South China Sea, reveal attraction was headed for Cambodia
  • 20,000 Hong Kong households face 2-day blackout after cable fire triggers power outages, class suspensions and disruptions to hospital and rail services
  • 76 per cent of young people polled identify as ‘Hongkongers’, while only 2 per cent think of themselves as ‘Chinese’
  • Häagen-Dazs Hong Kong apologises after banned pesticide found in 2 types of vanilla ice cream products
  • Hong Kong protests: 2 teenagers sentenced to correctional training, hard labour for vandalising bakery during 2019 protests
  • President Xi Jinping sends Hong Kong personal message ahead of 25th anniversary handover celebrations
  • Hong Kong and BN(O) passport holders can travel to South Korea visa-free again after restrictions eased, while city logs 1,198 Covid cases
  • When will China’s Fujian aircraft carrier be ready for active duty?
  • Boris Johnson’s father Stanley hopes UK parliament removes ban on Chinese ambassador
  • China supercomputer achieves global first with ‘brain-scale’ AI model
  • China-US relations: Joe Biden’s Asia-Pacific trade framework to be handled with ‘caution’, Beijing advisers say
  • Beijing sends 29 warplanes into Taiwan’s air defence zone in one of largest fly-bys of 2022
  • China plans to review every single social media comment, sparking more censorship fears
  • China, US urged to ‘start small’ as Biden ponders Trump-era trade tariff rollback amid record inflation
  • In Brussels, tram ads for 25th Hong Kong handover anniversary pulled after complaints
  • Uygur Forced Labour Prevention Act takes effect in US, banning imports from Xinjiang
  • Police officer fired and 5 under investigation over attack on women in Chinese city of Tangshan
  • Chinese warships appear to be sailing around Japan amid tensions
  • China trade: North Korea’s Chinese soybean imports surge as coronavirus outbreak ramps up food shortage fears
  • Chinese province offers more maternity leave to help avert population crisis
  • Qatar World Cup organisers reverse course on Taiwan name change
  • Does China’s demotion of its deputy foreign minister signal a rethink over Russia ties?
  • China’s southern provinces on alert as flooding breaks records while heat hits further north
  • Tencent’s WeChat bans public accounts from providing secondary trading services for NFTs
  • ‘We will not give up’: wife of jailed Chinese coal magnate vows to push for new trial after he loses appeal
  • COP15: China gives up host role with call for urgent meeting on biodiversity, UN says
  • Reef shark rescued from Singapore oil refinery
  • Filipino lawyer shot dead in US was ‘a case of mistaken identity’, official says
  • Why Asia wants more nuclear weapons – thanks to Russia’s war in Ukraine
  • Indonesian artists’ ‘anti-Semitic’ work removed from German show after upsetting Israel
  • Japan’s Kishida says talks with China key to regional peace amid rising maritime tensions
  • From Indonesia to Pacific, women less likely to have 2 Covid-19 vaccine shots than men
  • Singapore and South Korea report first monkeypox cases
  • Syed Saddiq faced ‘pressure’ by Malaysia’s PPBM, his parents say during corruption trial
  • South Korea successfully launches home-grown Nuri space rocket after failed attempt last year
  • Singapore unveils US$1 billion package as cost of living rises amid inflation, Ukraine war
  • Bangladesh, India scramble to help 9 million people hit by ‘worst floods in 122 years’
  • Earthquake hits Afghanistan, at least 280 dead
  • First delivery of German heavy weaponry arrives in Ukraine as Zelensky urges West for more, quickly
  • Fate of ancient Afghan Buddhist city in hands of Taliban, Chinese copper mine consortium
  • Most significant US gun legislation in decades clears hurdle in Senate
  • Inside Ukraine’s bombarded Luhansk region: ‘Everything is on fire’
  • Bill Cosby must pay US$500,000 for Playboy Mansion sex assault in 1975, US jury finds
  • Capitol riot hearings: election officials recount threats, pressure campaign to keep Trump in power
  • Thousands flock to Stonehenge for summer solstice, the longest day of the year
  • Ukraine strikes Black Sea oil platform used as Russian military installation
  • Top French court rules against burkinis in city’s pools
  • Ukraine war: Russia vows to inflict pain on Lithuania over Kaliningrad goods ‘blockade’
  • Ukraine war: Russia says captured US fighters may face death penalty
  • UK grinds to halt as biggest rail strike in 30 years strands thousands of commuters
  • Inside Ukraine’s secret, helicopter rescue missions: ‘We lost a lot of pilots’
  • CLP Power and HK Electric adapt to help Hong Kong realise 2050 carbon-neutral goal
  • UK developer backed by Hong Kong tycoon Henry Cheng eyes US$171 million in tokenisation plan for London residential tower
  • Hong Kong stock rebound on pause after recouping US$570 billion in market value since March, as health stocks lead decline
  • Private equity fund backed by the family of Hang Seng Bank’s co-founder bets big on China’s SMEs amid fundraising slump
  • Broker exodus: high rents and electronic trading have seen more than half of Hong Kong’s brokerages leave Central since 1997
  • Swire Properties pays HK$1.96 billion for 13,200 sq ft plot in Wan Chai, beating 20 rivals in fierce competition
  • Chinese consumers exhibit more caution and price sensitivity as zero-Covid strategy fans jobs and income uncertainty: survey
  • Singapore’s CapitaLand offers ‘buy now, pay later’ plan for Vietnamese residential project to entice Hong Kong investors
  • Alibaba sales for 618 shopping festival rise with growing appetite for camping, fishing goods
  • Ukraine conflict highlights the need to find better ways to feed the world
  • Turmoil in global market for blockchain-based DeFi products leaves investors holding the bag
  • Chinese EV start-up Li Auto launches L9, the ‘full-size SUV with no match on the market’
  • As it turns 22, CEO says HKEX must learn from tech firms to deliver new products and services at low cost
  • Didi apps ousted from China app stores for privacy reasons near return after ride-hailing firm’s NYSE delisting, sources say
  • Evergrande: defaulted developer gets delisting reminder as restructuring, probe on US$2 billion deposits leave investors in the dark
  • China’s climate policies are forcing renewable energy developers to incorporate costly storage facilities into new solar and wind projects
  • Microsoft scraps some AI facial-analysis tools, citing risk of bias
  • No 3-Michelin-star restaurants for Dubai as the food guide gives its first awards to the city
  • Scandinavian design from living room to bedrooms and kitchen turns Hong Kong family apartment into a future-proofed home
  • 5 Hong Kong influencers reveal vulnerable sides – and their tips to overcome tough times and achieve holistic wellness
  • What is skin purging and how is it different from a breakout? Experts explain how to understand what’s happening to your face
  • How volunteering benefits your mental health and self-esteem as well as others: Hong Kong volunteers’ stories
  • BTS taking a break is one of the biggest entertainment news stories of 2022. Why did it get so messy?
  • The Karate Kid musical’s Mr Miyagi on Asian representation, how it’s ‘not a movie being staged’, and stepping into Pat Morita’s shoes
  • Hong Kong Philharmonic thrill with vibrant Respighi and a pyrotechnic piccolo premiere
  • Documenta 2022: curated by Asians for first time, German art event’s highs and lows as opening week kicks off
  • Broker: Hirokazu Kore-eda, acclaimed Japanese director, on his first Korean film, starring Song Kang-ho, Gang Dong-won and IU
  • Who is Blackpink’s richest member? Net worths, ranked – Jennie, Lisa, Rosé and Jisoo work with Celine, Prada, Bulgari, Dior and Mac, raking in millions from Instagram and their music too
  • How much does Shah Rukh Khan charge to appear at events? The Bollywood legend performed at Isha Ambani’s wedding, but it could cost you a cool US$1 million to have him attend yours
  • Blue Ivy Carter and Harper Beckham at age 10: how do their childhoods compare? Besides famous parents Beyoncé and Jay-Z, and Posh and Becks, they are both budding fashionistas born into major wealth
  • Who is Jennifer Lopez’s gender-neutral teen, Emme Muñiz? The young singer uses they/them pronouns, has written a children’s book, and is close with twin Max and Ben Affleck’s kids Seraphina and Samuel
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Tailor remakes kimono left by the deceased into family keepsakes
  • Early bird hiking tour with breakfast offers best of Hiroshima
  • Lisa’s Wanderings Around Japan/Ise Jingu: Finding divine grace along with hospitality at a grand shrine

  • EDITORIAL: As the guardian of free trade, WTO has a duty to fix itself
  • VOX POPULI: Half-baked look at government’s COVID response led to bad report
  • VOX POPULI: The great closure of bookstores nationwide bodes ill for humanity
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
